characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adam Westing: Marven plays himself as a somewhat exagerrated presenter compared to his presence in more mundane documentaries. He is prone to improvising rescues and taking major safety risks. Badass Bookworm: He knows all about the prehistoric creatures he's about to face, and constantly tries to capture them. Most of the time, he succeeds. Expy: This of this version of him as atime-traveling BritishSteve Irwin. The Kirk: He's plenty snarky, though with a more jovial than sassing nature. He's also prone to emotional and logical choices in equal measure. Pragmatic Hero: In "Supercroc", he lures Matilda to the pond where theDeinosuchushe brought back earlier settled down, which snaps at her when she approaches; the situation was grave enough that even he was willing to take the very likely chance that Matilda would be eaten, or at least attacked.

characters.name: Nigel Marven
characters.description: Adam Westing: Marven plays himself as a somewhat exagerrated presenter compared to his presence in more mundane documentaries. He is prone to improvising rescues and taking major safety risks. Badass Bookworm: He knows all about the prehistoric creatures he's about to face, and constantly tries to capture them. Most of the time, he succeeds. Expy: This of this version of him as atime-traveling BritishSteve Irwin. The Kirk: He's plenty snarky, though with a more jovial than sassing nature. He's also prone to emotional and logical choices in equal measure. Pragmatic Hero: In "Supercroc", he lures Matilda to the pond where theDeinosuchushe brought back earlier settled down, which snaps at her when she approaches; the situation was grave enough that even he was willing to take the very likely chance that Matilda would be eaten, or at least attacked.

characters.name: Bob
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He's usually on the receiving end of whatever bad things the prehistoric creatures brought to the park get up to, usually from the Titanosaurs. Cool Old Guy: Despite his streak of bad luck, he's a wise advisor to Nigel and knows when to take action when things go wrong. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Jerk is an overstatement, but he's the only member of the main trio to be a bit fed up with some of the animals antics. Still, he loves and cares for them just as much as the others. The McCoy: He's the most emotional of the main trio, especially when he has to deal with the animals Nigel keeps bringing back. Only Sane Man: He often complains to Nigel whenever he's planning to bring dangerous creatures back to the park, or points out the potential issues that may come with these actions. He's still fond of the creatures, even the Titanosaurs, but will still point out issues they cause.

characters.name: Suzanne
characters.description: Hospital Hottie: Downplayed. She's a good-looking woman working as a veterinarian, but her attractiveness isn't put in any focus. The Spock: She's the calmest of the main trio, and the one who puts the most focus on her work. Team Mom: Especially in "Supercroc", where she had to nurse the new baby Sabretooths because their mother was unable to produce milk.

characters.name: Saba Douglas-Hamilton
characters.description: Friend to All Living Things: Just as much as Nigel, and it's all the more heartbreaking to her when the pair couldn't save the Sabretooth cubs. Guest-Star Party Member: She joins Nigel's second trip to prehistoric South America to and save the last Sabretooth cats in the world. Panthera Awesome: She's an expert on big cats, having done field work with lions, leopards, and cheetahs in Kenya.

characters.name: Theo
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: Despite only being a young subadult (at only 3 tons), he looks identical to the adultTriceratops. We now know that the ontogeny ofTriceratopswasa lot more complex. Granted, Theo's age group was already reasonably close in appearance to the adults. Demoted to Extra: Other than his introduction episode, he only has minor cameos. Pint-Sized Powerhouse: He starts out as a small dinosaur due to his young age, but he begins to grow rather quickly after his debut. Temper-Ceratops: A combination of territorial instincts and teenage hormones gives Theo an aggression problem. Bob deals with this by using tires and PVC tubing to disguise a tractor as anotherTriceratops, and uses it to stage fights with Theo so he can blow off steam in a controlled manner; becoming more prone to unleashing his fury on the vehicle instead of his fence and the parkkeepers.

characters.name: TheOrnithomimusflock
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: They're depicted as scaly and semiaquatic (to which Bob notes that they act more like ducks than ostriches), while the real ones were feathered and fully terrestrial. Ugly Cute: The babyOrnithomimusare gangly-looking with disproportionately large heads and eyes, but still manage to be adorable.

characters.name: Terrance and Matilda
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: They're significantly more slender as adults thanTyrannosauruswas in real life, barely changing from their juvenile forms. A Day in the Limelight: Terrance gets a whole subplot to himself in "Bug House", revolving around his being seriously injured and put on Death's door after a vicious fight with his sister. Big Sister Bully: Matilda is established as one since the end of the first episode, domineering over Terrance and outright trying to kill him in "Bug House". Even the narrator takes the time to note how her hostility towards her brother only seems to grow. Butt-Monkey: Terrance is the biggest animalisticButt-Monkeyin the series, given how much abuse he takes from his sister, even nearly getting killed by her at one point. Cain and Abel: A fight between the pair in "Dinobirds" ends with them being forcibly separated and a divider built inside their enclosure for Terrance's safety. "Bug House" takes it even further when Matilda breaks through to Terrance's side and nearly kills him.

characters.name: Martha
characters.description: A Day in the Limelight: Aside from her debut, "Supercroc" had her feeling isolated again after the herd's matriarch rejected her. During the breakout, she briefly go toe-to-toe with Matilda to save the resident baby of the herd, allowing her to regain her position among them. Gentle Giant: She's as sensitive and sweet-natured as a giant, hairy, elephant-like creature can get. Honorable Elephant: She's aGentle Giantwho stays very calm around the parkkeepers. When Nigel found her, she was seriously wounded by the hunters who killed her sister, but refused to leave the sister's body until Nigel gently coaxed her to follow him through the portal. Mama Bear: She's quite fond of the elephant herd's baby, even rushing to defend it from Matilda during the breakout. Too Unhappy to Be Hungry: After she's brought back to the park, Martha loses her appetite, putting her at the risk of her infection flaring up again. Nigel first assumes that she's being fed the wrong kind of plants, but it's only after he offers her some authentic Ice Age grass that he realizes she's a herd animal kept in solitary confinement, refusing to eat because of the loneliness. With Bob and Suzanne's help, he introduces her to the park's herd of African elephants, who accept her and allows her appetite to come back.

characters.name: TheElasmotherium
characters.description: Animal Stampede: During the breakout in "Supercroc", he's seen fleeing the approaching Matilda alongside theOrnithomimusflock. Dire Beast: It's an ice age rhino twice as big as any extant rhinoceros, being just marginally smaller than a woolly mammoth. Horn Attack: Especially when you have a2-meter hornattached to your skull. Rhino Rampage: Like modern rhinos, it has poor eyesight and will charge anything it perceives as a threat. Nigel uses that to get it to chase him through the portal and into the park. Rule of Cool: They could have brought back the woolly rhino (Coelodonta antiquitatis) featured inWalking with Beasts, butElasmotherium sibiricumwas twice as big and sported a 2-meter horn (though whether it had a horn or not has been debated).

characters.name: TheMicroraptorflock
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: Nigel implies thatMicroraptoris ancestral to birds. Unless he was speaking very, very broadly (emphasizing the dromaeosaurs and avians being sister taxa), this is just flat-out wrong, asMicroraptorwas a dromaeosaurid (the same family asVelociraptor,DeinonychusandUtahraptor) and thus couldn't possibly have been ancestral to birds. Not to mention that many true archaic birds (such as entantiornithines, jeholornithids and pygostylian) lived alongside it at Jiufotang. Anachronism Stew: Like several other media portrayals,Microraptoris erroneously shown coexisting with the fauna of the Yixian Formation, when it actually comes from the overlying Jiufotang Formation, thus making it at least several million years younger. Granted, similar microraptorians likeSinornithosaurusandChangyuraptorare known from Yixian. Informed Attribute: The show makes a big deal about what a "bizarre" dinosaur this was due to its very bird-like nature. In reality,Microraptorwas no more unusual or bird-like than any other dromaeosaurid such asVelociraptororDeinonychus, it was simply more arboreal and capable of gliding/possibly powered flight due to its smaller size (not all birds are small and volant either, like ratites and terror birds). The differences between microraptorians and eudromaeosaurs (the larger and terrestrial variety) isn't much greater than the differences between a housecat and a lion. Furthermore, it's shown coexisting withIncisivosaurus, which hasrodent-like buck teeth(a feature pretty much unheard of in any other dinosaur). Raptor Attack: Melanosomes found in the feathers ofMicroraptorfossils suggest it was completely black in color, instead of being largely white like the ones in the series.

characters.name: The Titanosaur Herd
characters.description: Gentle Giant: Despite their size and their tendency to destroy things, they're not dangerous.That changes in "Supercroc" when Bob, startled by the stowawayTroodon, nearly crashes his jeep into one of them, sending it on a frightened rampage where it breaks through several of the park's enclosures. The Millstone: Thanks to their size, strength, and obliviousness, they frequently cause destructive trouble for the park.Their first breakout in "Saving the Sabretooth" prevents Bob from hearing what size cage Nigel needs for the Terror Bird he intends to bring back.In "Bug House", one of them demolishes part of the titular building while it's under construction, setting Bob back on his schedule.In "Supercroc", another one knocks down the trees that Bob and the other keepers are planting as future food for them. A frightened one also causes the breakout near the end of the episode, though that can be seen as more of the stowawayTroodon's fault. Their first breakout in "Saving the Sabretooth" prevents Bob from hearing what size cage Nigel needs for the Terror Bird he intends to bring back. In "Bug House", one of them demolishes part of the titular building while it's under construction, setting Bob back on his schedule. In "Supercroc", another one knocks down the trees that Bob and the other keepers are planting as future food for them. A frightened one also causes the breakout near the end of the episode, though that can be seen as more of the stowawayTroodon's fault.

characters.name: The Sabretooths
characters.description: Always Someone Better: Even more so than inWalking with Beasts. Here, the arrival ofSmilodonin South America outright led to the extinction of terror birds via competitive exclusion.Though the actual fossil record in South America does not support this idea, instead suggesting that sabretooths simply filled up the apex predator niche following the earlier extinction of the terror birds. Artistic License – Paleontology: It overall looks more like a generic big cat with saber teeth rather thanSmilodon, with the skull in particular not being tall or narrow enough and its sabers are disproportionally small (S.populatorhad fangs up 30 cm long). It's especially weird when compared to its counterpart fromWalking with Beasts, which was pretty much a perfect restoration ofSmilodon populator. Crippling Overspecialization: Why it became extinct.Smilodon populatorwas a massive feline and big game specialist that thrived in South America while it was populated by giant herbivores likeToxodonandMacrauchenia, but once they disappeared atthe end of the last ice age, so didSmilodon, as it couldn't sustain itself on smaller game like deer. Foil: To cheetahs of all things. Both big cats are examples ofCrippling Overspecialization, but the cheetah is a lithe sprinter that can only hunt similarly gracile prey like gazelle and can't defend itself from tougher predators like lions and hyenas, whileSmilodonis a massive, stocky brawler that can't effectively hunt smaller, swifter prey. May–December Romance: The maleSmilodonthat gets brought back to the park is stated to be a young male, while the female is implied to be past her prime, or at least notably older than her partner. In the final episode, her milk dries up for unknown reasons, forcing the park's staff to bottlefeed her cubs.

characters.name: The Terror Bird
characters.description: A giant, flightless, carnivorous bird encountered by Nigel during his first trip to Pleistocene South America. Noting how its species was rendered extinct because they couldn't compete with the saber-toothed cats, Nigel uses a hunk ofToxodonmeat to save it, tying the meat to his jeep and letting the bird charge after it through the time portal. All Animals Are Domesticated: While hardly the only animal in the park to play this trope straight, it is the only predator that is shown acting rather chill around the humans, even when it escapes from its enclosure (more than once). By contrast, the only other predatory theropods at the parknoteBefore the unscheduled arrival of theTroodon, Terrence and Matilda, are treated as highly volatile and dangerous, requiring constant supervision (especially Matilda). Anachronism Stew: While not specifically identified asPhorusrhacosthis time, it's still shown living 1 million years ago, though at least in this case, it's made clear that terror birds are on the verge of extinction by this point. However, the last known occurrence of a giant phorusrhacid in South America dates to around 2 million years ago (a tarsometatarsus from the lower Raigon Formation of Uruguay), and the North AmericanTitanisvanished around the same time. Artistic License – Paleontology: While the design is definitely an improvement over the oversized seriema fromWalking with Beasts, with no wing claws and an appropriately large head, its legs and neck are still too long, once more leading to its standing 10 feet tall, when 7-8 feet would be more likely, due to giant phorusrhacids being very stocky animals. Escape Artist: "Supercroc" shows that the bird has developed a habit of escaping its enclosure, since it's fond of taking dust baths close to the fence and digging holes big enough for it to slip through.

characters.name: The Carboniferous Arthropods
characters.description: Big Creepy-Crawlies: All three of them look the part, as thePulmonoscorpiusis a feisty scorpion the size of a cat, theMeganeurais an active dragonfly with the wingspan of an eagle, and theArthropleurais a millipede about two meters long, tall enough to look a man right in the eye when reared on its hind legs. Notably, the insectophobic Bob tells the viewers that he isn't frightened by these guys, espescially theArthropleura, since they manage to win his sympathy because of their large sizes, making them appear as "proper animals" instead of tiny, wriggly insects. The Big Guy: It's the biggest of the three Carboniferous arthropods by a wide margin, but it's also a plant-eatingGentle Giant. Creepy Centipedes: Downplayed. It's certainly an alien and imposing sight but it's more bark than bite, as it just rears up as a threat display and then runs away from any perceived threat. In the present day, Bob grows rather fond of it, saying that its large size makes it a "proper animal" instead of a creepy little bug that crawls up your back. Shrouded in Myth: When he first meets one, Nigel mentions how we know little aboutArthropleura, including its diet. As it turns out, it's a docile herbivore who prefers threat displays over fighting. Rule of Cool: The various species ofArthropleuravaried greatly in size, with the smallest ones being less than 2 feet long. Naturally, the show features the very largest species ofArthropleurathat grows bigger than a man.

characters.name: TheDeinosuchus
characters.description: Ascended Extra: In a meta sense.Deinosuchusbriefly appeared in Impossible Pictures'Walking with Dinosaurs(specifically "Death of a Dynasty") as a floating, animatronic head. Now it's the star of its own episode, hunting hadrosaurs and pterosaurs and battling tyrannosaurs. Animals Not to Scale: Said to be 50 feet long, but most specimens ofDeinosuchus riograndensisare no more than 33-35 feet long, with some more fragmentary fossils suggesting a max length of 40 feet. The 50-foot figure is an outdated one based on the poorly reconstructed holotype skull of D.riograndensis. Animal Gender-Bender: The one brought to the park is identified as a female and yet she's as colossal as the otherDeinosuchusseen in the episode. Crocodilians, however, are very sexually dimorphic, with females being smaller than males, andDeinosuchuswould have likely been the same. Dire Beast: It's essentially a conventional crocodilian (even being a basal member of the extant alligatoroid lineage) but bigger than a T. rex. Everything Is Big in Texas: It even housed one of the largest crocodiles known from the fossil record.

characters.name: TheTroodon
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: The one seen here is depicted with a thin feathered coat, while the real animals are believed to have had a full covering of complex feathers. Hungry Menace: It's accidentally brought back to the park by stowing away on Nigel's jeep to eat his leftoverDeinosuchusbait. After the breakout is fixed, Bob manages to trap it in its own enclosure by enticing it with more meat. Misplaced Wildlife:Troodon(or ratherStenonychosaurus) is only conclusively known from Alberta and Montana, not Texas. Somewhat justified, as at the time,Troodonwas used as a massive wastebin taxon for any troodontid teeth and other fragments found throughout North America, from Alaska to Texas. Its cousinTalos(known from a single specimen from the far south of Utah) would be a better fit. Raptor Attack: Slit pupils, pronated hands, and a very thin coating of fuzz (making it look practically scaly). Just compare it to theTroodonfromDinosaur Planetwhich came out three years earlier. Given that the show'sMicroraptorandIncisivosauruswere decently featured (for the time), this is quite odd, especially with Nigel acknowledging that troodontids are close relatives of raptors. Scavengers Are Scum: They are depicted as sneaky opportunists who prove to be a major nuisance for Nigel while he's trying to capture aDeinosuchus, as they keep stealing the meat Nigel plans to use as bait.

characters.name: Terrance and Matilda’s mother
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: Just like her children and otherT.rexesin the show, she’s a lot more slender than the real thing. Behemoth Battle: She gets into a brutal fight with a male Tyrannosaurus that was after her kill. Despite putting up a good fight even with an injured leg, she ends up fatally injured after her head gets slammed against a rock. Handicapped Badass: Even after getting gored in the left thigh by aTriceratops, she still manages to hunt and kill anOrnithomimus(a species of dinosaur that usually runs too fast for even ahealthy T.rexto catch) to feed to her chicks. When a rival male attacks Terrance and Matilda, the still-injured mother fights the male to the best of her ability, before he kills her by smashing her head into a rock. Mama Bear: She defends her young from a rival male despite being greatly injured, but the battle unfortunately costs her her life. She also spends the final moments of her life roaring at Nigel in a final attempt at defending her offspring. Mighty Roar: She lets out a long and loud one at Nigel as he approached her fallen body.

characters.name: Cave Bear
characters.description: Anachronistic Animal: In-universe example, as Nigel is surprised to find a cave bear 10,000 years ago, since the species is thought to have died out 24,000 years ago. This emphasizes the incompleteness of the fossil record, and that some animals could have had longer temporal ranges than what the available material suggests (ironically this wound up being the case for theElasmotheriumfrom the same episode, who got its extinction date pushed back by over a hundred thousand years!) Animals Not to Scale: It's stated to stand 13 feet tall, meaning4 meters, but the largest specimens ofUrsus spelaeuswould have only stood 3 meters tall on their hind legs. The given weight of three quarters of a ton, however, is pretty accurate for a decent-sized male cave bear (and too low for one supposedly standing 4 meters tall). Bears Are Bad News: Played with. He does chase after Nigel but only after Nigel disturbs him from his hibernation and after Nigel escapes up a tree, the bear calmly returns to his cave to resume hibernating. No One Gets Left Behind: Averted, as it's one of the few animals Nigel doesn't consider bringing back, as he's focused on saving the last of the mammoths. Though the fact thatcave bears are essentially just bigger versions of the brown bearand thus less exotic than a mammoth or woolly rhino might have had something to do with it. Vegetarian Carnivore: Nigel states that they are not very carnivorous bears, which isTruth in Television, as cave bears are thought to be even more herbivorous than their brown bear relatives (in the latter's case, meat usually only makes up 10% of their diet).

characters.name: Incisivosaurus
characters.description: Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Easily the most vibrantly colorful dinosaur in any Impossible Pictures paleo-documentary. Covers Always Lie: One promotional image shows it being attacked by theMei longbut the two theropods never actually interact in the episode. The closest we get is Nigel cutting his first encounter with theIncisivosaurusshort after hearing one of his teammates being attacked by theMei long. Goofy Buckteeth: Yes, it's an actual dinosaur with rodent-like gnashers. Goofy Feathered Dinosaur: Downplayed, but between its garish colorations,Goofy Buckteeth, acting like an angry rooster, and the quirky music playing whenever it's on-screen, it's clearly not being taken too seriously. Rule of Cool: They could have used the relatedCaudipteryxbut it didn't haveGoofy Buckteeth.

characters.name: Mei long
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: It's depicted as wolf-sized here, when they were actually the size of a ferret. The tiny holotype ofMei longwas thought to be an immature specimen but not a hatchling, and indeed, a second specimen further confirmed that this was a basal dwarf species of troodontid.Sinocalliopteryx(named just a year after the series aired) would have been a much better fit. Palette Swap: Shares the same model as theTroodonand even its color scheme and body patterns are night-identical (simply being olive green in color). Raptor Attack: Being aPalette Swapof theTroodon, it likewise has slit pupils, broken wrists, and only a very thin coat of filaments. And much like theTrope Codifier(Jurassic Park'sVelociraptor), it's gratuitously oversized. Underground Monkey: Basically identical to theTroodonin every way, except that theMeiare evidently bolder, having no trouble mugging alien visitors like humans for food, while theTroodonare more skittish and prefers stealing the human's food behind their back.

characters.name: Pterosaur
characters.description: Anachronism Stew: By virtue of being a repurposedNyctosaurusit resembles a pteranodontian (and a large one) but we have no evidence of pteranodontians existing as far back as the early Aptian (their earliest known occurrence dates to the late Turonian). We've pretty much found every other type of Cretaceous pterosaur at Early Cretaceous Liaoningbut them. Granted, at the time, several of the local taxa likeChaoyangopterusand the not-so-apply namedEopteranodonwere thought to be pteranodontians. Animals Not to Scale: If it's indeed meant to beChaoyangopterus, which only had a 6-foot wingspan, while these ones arePteranodon-sized. Giant Flyer: We see one swooping over Nigel, showing that they arePteranodon-sized. No Name Given: It's never identified as a specific genus, though the fact that it shares the same model as theNyctosaurusminus the extravagant head crest implies that they are meant to beChaoyangopterus, which coexisted withMicroraptorat the Jiufotang Formation and was originally identified as a nyctosaurid upon its description in 2003, though it was identified as an azhdarchoid by the timePrehistoric Parkaired and later became the type genus of its own family, Chaoyangopteridae. In the early days of the fandom, it was sometimes labeled asEosipterus(now thought to be a ctenochasmatoid or germanodactyloid) but there is no source verifying this. Palette Swap: It's the same model as theNyctosaurusbut colored brown and lacking a head-crest.

characters.name: Toxodon
characters.description: Angry, Angry Hippos: Essentially depicted as a South American equivalent to a hippo. They are placid if left alone but when provoked, they can be dangerous. One enraged bull charges Nigel when the latter follows the herd to their waterhole. Artistic License – Paleontology: "Saving the Sabretooth" follows the older interpretation thatToxodonspent a lot of time wallowing in water like hippos, but that ideas has been criticized by later researchers since the morphology ofToxodonmore closely resembles a terrestrial rhino than an amphibious hippo and its fossils are usually found in more arid environments. The episode does at least show large herds of them grazing the open plains during the daytime (while hippos only do it at night). Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Though resembling hippos and rhinos in shape, instead of being a dull gray,Toxodonhas a more striking brown and white color pallet complete with dark eye markings and stripes on its rumps, resembling a mix between a giant panda and an okapi. It helps to highlight its independent evolution on the isolated continent of South America. Faster Than They Look: Like hippos and rhinos,Toxodonlooks deceptively slow due to its plump and squat physique but is actually fast enough to keep up with a jeep. Herbivores Are Friendly: Averted. A bullToxodonattacks Nigel, forcing the latter to escape in his jeep, which theToxodoncan keep up with until Nigel hits the gas. Nigel says thatToxodonare just as dangerous as aSmilodon.

characters.name: Crassigyrinus
characters.description: Amphibian at Large: Not a true amphibian but it looks the part, being a man-sized and amphibious predator that resembles a cross between a salamander and a tadpole. Anachronism Stew: Along withPulmonoscorpius, it's actually known from the upper Mississippian, not the end of the Pennsylvanian, thus having lived over 25 million years before the setting of "The Bug House". Artistic License – Paleontology: It's depicted with a tall, tadpole-like head but late research revealed that this restoration of its skull was erroneous, and its head was actually wider and flatter, like many other similar amphibious stem-tetrapods from the time. Rule of Cool: Averted. There were other amphibious tetrapods/stem-tetrapods during the Late Carboniferous that grew as big as American alligators but the show used the more modestly sizedCrassigyrinus, as it is native to Scotland. Swamp Monster: A large, amphibious predator that hunts in swampy waters, and it even goes unseen during its first appearance. Nigel literally nicknames it "The Swamp Monster".

characters.name: Albertosaurus
characters.description: Anachronism Stew: Depending on what you class asAlbertosaurus. The type species (A.sarcophagus) is only known from the early Maastrichtian, but the closely relatedGorgosaurus libratus(orAlbertosaurus libratus) did live during the late Campanian,though only further north in Alberta and Montana. Artistic License – Paleontology:Being aPalette Swapof the show's T. rex model, some anatomical inaccuracies are inevitable. While the gracile frame and long legs suitAlbertosaurusbetter than an adultTyrannosaurus, the skull more closely matches the latter than the former, and lacks the pronounced brow-horns typical of albertosaurines.They are also said to be "early ancestors of T. rex". While both are tyrannosaurids,Tyrannosauruswas part of the tyrannosaurine subfamily, whileAlbertosaurusbelonged to the albertosaurines. It's akin to saying that cougars are ancestors of lions. There is also the fact thatAlbertosaurusand earlier representatives ofTyrannosaurus(like T.mcraeensis) were actually contemporaries during the latest Campanian-lower Maastrichtian (granted, this wasn't known at the time). Being aPalette Swapof the show's T. rex model, some anatomical inaccuracies are inevitable. While the gracile frame and long legs suitAlbertosaurusbetter than an adultTyrannosaurus, the skull more closely matches the latter than the former, and lacks the pronounced brow-horns typical of albertosaurines. They are also said to be "early ancestors of T. rex". While both are tyrannosaurids,Tyrannosauruswas part of the tyrannosaurine subfamily, whileAlbertosaurusbelonged to the albertosaurines. It's akin to saying that cougars are ancestors of lions. There is also the fact thatAlbertosaurusand earlier representatives ofTyrannosaurus(like T.mcraeensis) were actually contemporaries during the latest Campanian-lower Maastrichtian (granted, this wasn't known at the time). Informed Attribute: Said to be more light-weight than T. rex. While true, this is undermined by the fact that the twoshare the

characters.name: Parasaurolophus
characters.description: Advertised Extra: The opening sequence shows a pair living in the park, with Nigel's initial encounter with two juveniles seeming to be a setup to rescue them, but ultimatelyParasaurolophusis the only species from the intro that Nigel never rescued.noteNyctosaurusisn't rescued either, but has the excuse that making an aviary for them would likely be beyond the park's logistics. Herbivores Are Friendly: Played straight. They are shown to be very docile but also high-strung and ready to book it at any sign of danger.Nigel can even safely approach two younger individuals and pet them on the snout. Misplaced Wildlife: The only dinosaur in "Super Croc" to aver it. Its appearance and large size (said to be over 10 meters) are a perfect match forParasaurolophus tubicen, the largest and last species ofParasaurolophus, whose fossils have been found in New Mexico, which neighbors Texas. Monster Munch: They are given some scenes to themselves (notably the "Dr. Dolittle" bit) but their main contribution is to be prey for theDeinosuchusandAlbertosaurus, and when they are not being preyed upon, they are shown fleeing from danger. Outliving One's Offspring: A juvenileParasaurolophusgets ambushed and dragged into the water by aDeinosuchus, who proceeds to tear it apart by performing the "death roll" and is joined by two conspecifics.The mother can do nothing but honk in distress from the bank.

characters.name: Nyctosaurus
characters.description: Artistic License – Paleontology: Like most of the pterosaurs from theWalking with... series, it too is shown hunting fish by skim-feeding (a theory that was heavily criticized by later researchers) and lacks pycnofibers. Awesome, but Impractical: As the narrator states, its towering head-crest is purely there for display like a peacock's tail. It doesn't make it any more maneuverable or aerodynamic. Anachronism Stew: The most notable example in "Super Croc", asNyctosaurusfossils are only known from Santonian-aged rocks in the Niobrara Formation (nearly 10 million years before the setting of "Super Croc"). Granted, nyctosaurids as a whole survived until the K-Pg extinction. Historically, a partial humerus from the Maastrichtian of Brazil was attributed toNyctosaurus, thus greatly expanding its temporal range, but later studies found this specimen to more closely align withBarbaridactylus(who matches its age and larger size better). Monster Munch: One of them meets its end getting snatched by aDeinosuchusmid-flight. Rule of Cool: Played with. They could have used its much larger and more famous cousinPteranodonbutNyctosaurushad a far more outlandish head crest.
